title: Cloud Assets & File Handling

questions.question_text: Why shouldn't you store large image files directly in your main SQL or Mongo database?
options: Images are too colorful for databases
It significantly degrades database performance and increases backup sizes
Databases can only store binary numbers
questions.explanation: Databases are optimized for structured text and numbers. Large files should be kept in specialized 'Object Storage' like S3 or Cloudinary.

questions.question_text: Why should you NOT store media files (images/videos) directly inside your database?
options: Databases are for text; binary files make databases huge, slow, and expensive to backup
questions.explanation: Standard practice is: store the FILE on a storage service and store the URL in the database.

options: A cloud-based image and video management service with built-in optimization and transformation
questions.explanation: Cloudinary handles resizing and cropping images on-the-fly via URLs.

options: A scalable object storage service used for storing any type of file
questions.explanation: S3 is the industry standard for bulk file storage.

questions.question_text: What does a 'File Upload' workflow look like in MERN?
options: React -> Express (Multer) -> Cloud Provider -> DB (Save URL)
questions.explanation: The backend usually acts as a 'bridge' to securely upload files.

questions.question_text: Which Node.js middleware is standard for handling 'multipart/form-data' (file uploads)?
questions.explanation: Multer adds a `.file` or `.files` object to the request.

options: Changing an image's size, format, or quality by modifying its URL (e.g., adding /w_500/)
questions.explanation: This saves bandwidth by only serving the exact size the user needs.

options: A network of servers globally that cache your images so they load fast for users everywhere
questions.explanation: Cloudinary and S3 (with CloudFront) act as CDNs.

questions.question_text: What is the danger of allowing any file type to be uploaded?
options: The site will be too colorful
Security; an attacker could upload an executable script (.js, .php) and run it on your server
questions.explanation: ALWAYS filter file types (Allow only .jpg, .png, etc.).

questions.question_text: How do you show a 'Preview' of an image in React before it is uploaded?
options: Using URL.createObjectURL(file) to get a temporary local URL for the tag
questions.explanation: This creates a great user experience by showing what will be uploaded.

options: A binary-to-text encoding scheme that allows you to send file data as a string in a JSON body
questions.explanation: Base64 is useful for small icons or quick uploads, but makes files 33% larger.

options: Only loading images as they enter the user's viewport (on scroll)
questions.explanation: This significantly improves page load speed for sites with many images.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of the 'alt' tag on an image?
options: Accessibility for screen readers and as fallback text if the image fails to load
questions.explanation: Good for SEO and for users with disabilities.

options: A temporary URL that gives limited-time access to a private file without making the whole bucket public
questions.explanation: Essential for selling digital files or private user documents.

options: A modern image format that provides superior lossless and lossy compression for images on the web
questions.explanation: WebP images are much smaller than PNG or JPG equivalents.

questions.question_text: How do you delete a file from Cloudinary in Node.js?
options: By deleting the URL from the DB
By using the Cloudinary SDK's `destroy(public_id)` method
questions.explanation: Clearing the URL from the DB is not enough; you must also remove the file from storage to save space.
